metadata.note: NEW atomic. Japan's posture on Taiwan and China's response -- distinct from taiwan_strait_theater, which is gated {ASIA-TAIWAN} only and covers the China<->Taiwan<->US axis. Terrain here is the Japan-China bilateral rupture; Taiwan is the trigger, not the theater. Dyad gate centroid_ids={ASIA-CHINA} + primary_target=ASIA-JAPAN. 'Taiwan' is legitimate Pillar-2 geography here (vocab spec Pillar 2 edge case) because Taiwan is NOT this FN's gated centroid -- 80/80 in-gate Taiwan titles are on-topic. Takaichi is a PRINCIPAL actor of the Japan side, not a third-party leader (hard rule 4 does not apply). Residual overlap with taiwan_military_pressure (27 PLA-drill titles) is accepted per spec 1. DROPPED post-audit (real-gate check): 'protest' (22 titles, but it is a generic diplomatic verb cutting across all five atomics -- it was pulling 'China lodges stern protest over Japan PM's Yasukuni Shrine tribute' from memory_wars and 'Japan protests Chinese drilling rig' from the maritime atomic; 'embassy' already catches the rupture titles, so recall loss is zero), 'envoy' (13, the vehicle for any Chinese FM statement), 'consulate' (5, mostly consular/citizen-safety noise), 'summon' (0 real hits), 'Taipei' (1 hit, off-topic).

bundle.term: Taiwan as the subject of the dispute
The legal and doctrinal frame of Japan's involvement
The diplomatic rupture and its incidents
